summary: Linked the item object `it_2F28` by replacing its 269-line assembly stub with a matching C implementation, switching build configuration from asm/nonmatching to source/matching, and adding the needed item data structures, forward declarations, union member, and corrected prototypes.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/it/items/it_2F28.s` and added a 133-line C implementation in `src/melee/it/items/it_2F28.c`.
Changed `obj_files.mk` so the object list uses `src/melee/it/items/it_2F28.c.o` instead of `asm/melee/it/items/it_2F28.s.o`.
Implemented exported functions `it_802F28C8`, `it_802F295C`, and `it_802F2BDC`, plus static item-state helpers `it_802F2A58`, `it_802F2AB0`, `it_802F2AE8`, and `it_802F2BA8`.
Moved the state table `it_803F9470` from assembly `.data` into C as an `ItemStateTable` array containing callbacks for life timer, physics/update, and collision logic.
Added `it_2F28_ItemVars` with one `f32 x0` field and `struct it_2F28_DatAttrs` with `Vec3 pos`, `Vec3 vel`, `f32 float1`, and `f32 float2` in `itCommonItems.h`.
Added `it_2F28_ItemVars it_2F28` to the `Item::xDD4_itemVar` union in `src/melee/it/types.h`.
Added forward declaration `typedef struct it_2F28_DatAttrs it_2F28_DatAttrs;` in `src/melee/it/forward.h`.
Updated `it_2F28.h` to expose `float it_802F295C(HSD_GObj* owner_gobj, it_2F28_DatAttrs* attr)` and to change `it_802F2BDC`'s second parameter from `Item_GObj*` to `HSD_GObj*`.
Updated `it_2725.h` prototype for `it_802758D4` from unknown return/params to `float it_802758D4(Item_GObj* gobj)`.
smart_moves: Linked the object cleanly by updating both build systems visible in the slice: `configure.py` status and `obj_files.mk` source-vs-asm object path.
Introduced narrow data types for the object-specific item variables and spawn attributes instead of leaving the code dependent on raw offsets.
Used `Item::xDD4_itemVar.it_2F28.x0` to name the former `0xDD4` item-var storage and preserve access through the existing per-item-vars union pattern.
Corrected nearby prototypes (`it_802758D4`, `it_802F2BDC`) to make the new C call sites type-check and communicate return/argument intent.
Kept internal state callbacks static while exporting only the entry points and `it_803F9470` state table through the item header.
Recreated the assembly's spawn setup through the existing `SpawnItem` API, including owner fields, position/velocity copies, `It_Kind_Unk2`, facing direction, damage, and flag initialization.
Used `sqrtf(SQ(x) + SQ(y))` for the horizontal velocity magnitude calculation that corresponded to the assembly's reciprocal-square-root sequence.
decomp_lessons: When linking an item file, update the object list and matching status in lockstep with deleting the asm file; otherwise the build may still consume the old assembly object.
Item-specific scratch fields at `xDD4` should be modeled as a named union member in `Item::xDD4_itemVar` once the owning item behavior is understood enough.
Spawn helper functions often need a small data-attribute struct mirroring copied position, velocity, and tuning floats from caller-provided data.
Replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` with precise prototypes can be required by a new matching C translation; here `it_802758D4` is evidenced as returning the float stored in `var_f31`.

State table callbacks can be brought over directly from assembly `.data` once static helper signatures are known, but function pointer parameter types may require casts or careful `Item_GObj`/`HSD_GObj` choices.
Stack padding such as `PAD_STACK(4)` can be part of a matching solution for spawn-heavy functions with local structs.
Do not over-name unknown item data prematurely: this PR used conservative names like `it_2F28_DatAttrs`, `float1`, `float2`, and `x0` where semantics were not established in the evidence.
naming_conventions: File and symbol naming stayed address-based for unknown item code: `it_2F28.c`, `it_802F28C8`, `it_802F295C`, `it_803F9470`.
Unknown item-specific structs followed the project pattern `it_2F28_ItemVars` and `it_2F28_DatAttrs`.
Unclear fields were kept generic (`x0`, `float1`, `float2`) rather than assigned speculative gameplay names.
Function prototypes in headers retained address comments such as `/* 2F295C */` and `/* 2F2BDC */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the assembly state-table data into `ItemStateTable it_803F9470[] = { { 0, it_802F2AB0, it_802F2AE8, it_802F2BA8 }, };`.
Matched the spawn function by using a local `SpawnItem spawn`, assigning copied `Vec3` values, setting `spawn.prev_pos.z = 0.0f`, then copying `spawn.pos = spawn.prev_pos`.
Preserved the returned float behavior in `it_802F295C`: initialize to `0.0f`, spawn the item, and only replace it with `it_802758D4(item_gobj)` if spawning succeeded.
Modeled the timer callback as decrementing `item->xD44_lifeTimer` by `1.0f` and returning true when it reaches or falls below zero.
Modeled horizontal speed limiting with `sqrtf(SQ(item->x40_vel.x) + SQ(item->x40_vel.y))` and scaled the X/Y velocity components only when above the stored threshold.

Used existing item helpers from the assembly call graph: `it_8027B4A4`, `it_8027B378`, `it_8027CE44`, `Item_8026A8EC`, `Item_80268B18`, `it_80275158`, `it_8026B3A8`, `Item_80268E5C`, `it_8026DFB0`, and `it_8026B894`.
The C includes both `<math.h>` and `<math_ppc.h>`, plus `<MetroTRK/intrinsics.h>`, indicating matching-sensitive math/intrinsics context.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no direct reviewer rationale in the evidence; lessons are inferred only from the title, changed files, and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `it_2F28_DatAttrs.float1`, `float2`, or `it_2F28_ItemVars.x0`.
Search for callers of `it_802F295C` to infer the gameplay identity of `It_Kind_Unk2` / `it_2F28`.
Check whether later cleanup removes the commented alternative prototypes around `it_802F2BA8`.
Look for other item link PRs using `PAD_STACK` with `SpawnItem` for matching stack layout.
Search for `it_802758D4(Item_GObj*)` updates to confirm the prototype remained stable.
